repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: ## Description This PR implements a mechanism to override terminfo capabilities at the stage of TUI initialization using environment variables as a request for #37274. ## Motivation Neovim initializes the TUI like `enter_ca_mode` **before** user configuration (`init.lua`) is loaded. Users cannot disable the **alternate screen** via Lua config. This PR provides a solution by checking the environment during the TUI startup phase. ## Implementation Added `apply_t_overrides()` in `src/nvim/tui/tui.c`. - it runs immediately after nvim's internal terminfo augmentation. - it iterates through known terminfo capabilities. - it looks for `env var` with the pattern `NVIM_T_[CAPABILITY]` ## Example User can disable alternate screen in shell config: ```bash export NVIM_T_ENTER_CA_MODE="DISABLE" export NVIM_T_EXIT_CA_MODE="DISABLE" ```

comment_regular_data.body: Removed test in `test/functional/terminal/tui_spec.lua`, all checks passed, ready for review.

Thank you for the the clean, simple, well-explained PR. 

However this approach seems like the wrong direction, because this basically reinvents Vim's `t_xx` options, with an even more quirky interface (envvar-based DSL).

As discussed in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/37274 supporting `$TERMINFO` is probably "doomed" since we are trying to get rid of terminfo, but we could instead support a `g:terminfo` dict.

Users can also create their own terminfo entries (based on an existing one but with some capabilities modified) and set TERM to that when starting nvim. No changes required for Nvim at all.

It’s kind of an obscure use case. Given it can be solved without Nvim’s involvement (and as Justin mentioned we want *less* terminfo machinery, not more), I’d personally advocate directing users to do that instead of changing Nvim.

> Users can also create their own terminfo entries (based on an existing one but with some capabilities modified) and set TERM to that when starting nvim. No changes required for Nvim at all.

Unfortunately that is blocked for Windows users https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/37274 . And there's still the question of how users will do this if/when we drop terminfo support entirely.
This has been closed since a request for information has not been answered for 30 days. It can be reopened when the requested information is provided.

performed_via_github_app.description: Automate your workflow from idea to production
performed_via_github_app.name: GitHub Actions

comment_regular_data.body:
> And there's still the question of how users will do this if/when we drop terminfo support entirely.

If terminfo support is dropped entirely, then the options I see are:
- Modify nvim source for `terminfo_builtin.h` and recompile
- Store terminfo overrides in e.g. a special lua file that's sourced during startup
- Env var(s)
- New command line arg(s)

I don't think it's an option to set it in normal config because it will likely be needed during TUI startup.

If terminfo is dropped, does that mean nvim will ship with a lot more builtin defs? Or is there some other plan?

> If terminfo is dropped, does that mean nvim will ship with a lot more builtin defs?

if we are missing defs from xterm/ncurses then we can add them. we can also hardcode "fixes" on top of that.

> Store terminfo overrides in e.g. a special lua file that's sourced during startup

still tricky for remote scenarios ...


Maybe the `NVIM_T_<NAME>` approach of this PR is ok. Or maybe a single `NVIM_TERMINFO` or `nvim --terminfo` which contains a JSON dict.

> Or maybe a single NVIM_TERMINFO or nvim --terminfo which contains a JSON dict.

Do we really want to use the JSON format for this? To me, It also feels like a bit awkward way to configure.

On linux/macOS, the env variable `$TERMINFO` refers to a terminfo file/directory path rather than the serialized entries; In light of this, I'm not sure if this is the best variable name.

> Do we really want to use the JSON format for this? To me, It also feels like a bit awkward way to configure.

What else do you suggest? `NVIM_T_<NAME>` is also awkward, and will require more special-purpose code for users.

title: feat(tui): allow env var overrides for terminfo capabilities
